steps.title: Find angle L in radians
steps.content:
Angle L is $15$ times angle K. Multiply the given measure of angle K by $15$, then simplify.
$$15(\frac{\pi}{2(15)})=\frac{15\pi}{30}=\frac{\pi}{2}$$

steps.title: Convert radians to degrees
steps.content:
Since $\pi$ radians equals $180^\circ$, half of $\pi$ radians equals half of $180^\circ$.
$$\frac{\pi}{2}=\frac{180^\circ}{2}=90^\circ$$

steps.title: Match the degree measure
steps.content: The measure of angle L is $90^\circ$. The choice with that degree measure is the correct one. The answer is D.
